gaps: A working CourtListener MCP server (the configured one fails at startup with a missing session-store env var; REST fallback works but bypasses harness-side retrieval logging)
A leakage-safe appellate-docket view (pre-decision CA10 entries, briefing, panel composition); CourtListener held nothing for this appeal but the termination entry
A date-bounded retrieval mode that filters this case's post-event docket entries server-side, so listing the district docket cannot incidentally surface post-decision entry descriptions
Corpus priors for circuit-appeal events on point (the ca10 cut is 34 mostly-'other' rows with no employment-trial analogue; topic filter '3442 Civil Rights: Jobs' returned 0)
helpful: RECAP full text of the district court's Rule 59 denial (recap-documents plain_text) — the single most informative input, mapping every likely appellate issue and its standard of review
District docket entry descriptions (procedural history: SJ denied, 5-day jury trial, defense verdict, new-trial denial, timely NOA)
fedcourts query ranged backend (ran fine, though results were off-point)
notes: Forward-mode cell on an already-terminated appeal (see flags.json); treated as de facto replay. The prediction leans on the district court's post-trial order rather than corpus priors, which had no analogue for this posture.
tools_used: CourtListener REST API v4 (direct; MCP server failed)
CourtListener MCP search (2 attempts, both errored)
